meta.title: Docker Error Reference
meta.description: Structured reference of Docker error codes, exit codes, daemon errors, and fixes.

errors.name: Generic Application Error
errors.message: Container exited with code 1
errors.cause: The application inside the container exited with a non-zero status. Usually an unhandled exception, missing config, or startup failure.
errors.fix: Run `docker logs <container>` to see the application's stdout/stderr. Check entrypoint script, env vars, and application startup sequence.

errors.name: OOM Kill / SIGKILL
errors.message: Container exited with code 137 (128 + SIGKILL)
errors.cause: Process received SIGKILL. Either the container exceeded its memory limit and was killed by the OOM killer, or the container was manually stopped with `docker kill`.
errors.fix: Increase memory limit with `--memory` flag. Profile the application for memory leaks. Check `docker inspect <container>` for OOMKilled: true.

errors.name: Segmentation Fault (SIGSEGV)
errors.message: Container exited with code 139 (128 + SIGSEGV)
errors.cause: The process attempted to access memory it was not permitted to access. Common in C/C++ applications, corrupted binaries, or incompatible base images.
errors.fix: Check for architecture mismatch (arm64 vs amd64). Rebuild the image for the target platform. Run with `--platform linux/amd64` if needed.

errors.name: Graceful Shutdown (SIGTERM)
errors.message: Container exited with code 143 (128 + SIGTERM)
errors.cause: Container received SIGTERM and exited cleanly. This is the expected behaviour when `docker stop` is called.
errors.fix: No action needed if this is expected. If unexpected, check orchestration system (Kubernetes, ECS) for evictions or rolling restarts.

errors.name: Command Not Found
errors.message: Container exited with code 127
errors.cause: The command specified in CMD or ENTRYPOINT was not found in the container. Missing binary or incorrect path.
errors.fix: Verify the entrypoint binary exists in the image: `docker run --rm <image> which <binary>`. Check spelling and PATH in the Dockerfile.

errors.name: Permission Denied on Entrypoint
errors.message: Container exited with code 126
errors.cause: The entrypoint script or binary exists but is not executable.
errors.fix: Add `RUN chmod +x /entrypoint.sh` in the Dockerfile before the ENTRYPOINT instruction.

errors.name: Misuse of Shell Builtin
errors.message: Container exited with code 2
errors.cause: Shell command misuse or incorrect usage of a shell builtin. Also returned by some applications to indicate a general usage error.
errors.fix: Check your CMD or ENTRYPOINT for syntax errors. Inspect logs with `docker logs <container>`.

errors.name: Image Pull Failed
errors.message: Error response from daemon: pull access denied / repository does not exist
errors.cause: Image name is misspelled, tag does not exist, or registry authentication failed.
errors.fix: Verify the image name and tag with `docker pull <image>:<tag>`. Login to private registries with `docker login <registry>`. Check network connectivity to the registry.

errors.name: Image Manifest Unknown
errors.message: Error response from daemon: manifest unknown
errors.cause: The specified tag does not exist in the registry, or the image was deleted after being referenced.
errors.fix: Check available tags on Docker Hub or your registry. Use `docker images` to check local cache. Use `latest` or a specific valid tag.

errors.name: Image Platform Mismatch
errors.message: WARNING: The requested image's platform (linux/arm64) does not match the detected host platform (linux/amd64)
errors.cause: Pulling an image built for a different CPU architecture (e.g. ARM image on AMD64 host or vice versa).
errors.fix: Build multi-platform images with `docker buildx`. Pull with `--platform linux/amd64` flag. Use `DOCKER_DEFAULT_PLATFORM=linux/amd64` env var.

errors.name: Port Already Allocated
errors.message: Bind for 0.0.0.0:3000 failed: port is already allocated
errors.cause: Another process or container is already bound to the same host port.
errors.fix: Run `lsof -i :3000` or `ss -tulpn | grep 3000` to find the conflicting process. Stop the other container or use a different host port: `-p 3001:3000`.

errors.name: Network Not Found
errors.message: Error response from daemon: network <name> not found
errors.cause: The specified Docker network does not exist, usually because `docker network create` was not run or the network was deleted.
errors.fix: Create the network: `docker network create <name>`. In Compose files, declare the network in the `networks:` section.

errors.name: DNS Resolution Failure
errors.message: Could not resolve host: <hostname>
errors.cause: Container cannot resolve DNS names. Common when containers on different networks try to communicate by service name.
errors.fix: Ensure all communicating containers are on the same Docker network. Use `--network` flag or define shared networks in docker-compose.yml. Check `/etc/resolv.conf` inside the container.

errors.name: No Space Left on Device
errors.message: Error response from daemon: write /var/lib/docker/...: no space left on device
errors.cause: Docker data directory has filled the host disk. Common after many builds accumulate unused layers and stopped containers.
errors.fix: Run `docker system prune -af` to remove unused images, containers, and build cache. Check disk usage with `df -h` and `docker system df`.

errors.name: Volume Mount Failed
errors.message: Error response from daemon: invalid mount config for type bind: source path does not exist
errors.cause: The host path specified in a bind mount does not exist, or Docker Desktop does not have permission to access it.
errors.fix: Create the host directory before running the container. On Docker Desktop (Mac/Windows), ensure the path is in the File Sharing list under Preferences.

errors.name: Build Context Too Large
errors.message: Sending build context to Docker daemon  2.5GB
errors.cause: The build context sent to the daemon includes large files (node_modules, .git, datasets) that should be excluded.
errors.fix: Add a `.dockerignore` file in the build directory. Exclude `node_modules`, `.git`, `*.log`, build artifacts, and large data files.

errors.name: RUN Command Failed During Build
errors.message: The command '/bin/sh -c <command>' returned a non-zero code: 1
errors.cause: A RUN instruction in the Dockerfile failed. Common causes: package not found, wrong base image version, network issues during apt/pip install.
errors.fix: Run the failing command interactively: `docker run --rm -it <base-image> bash`. Check package names and versions. Add `--no-cache` to the build to skip stale layers.

errors.name: Unexpected Cache Invalidation
errors.message: Slow builds due to cache being invalidated too early
errors.cause: COPY or ADD instructions placed before RUN apt/pip install force reinstallation on every code change.
errors.fix: Order Dockerfile instructions from least-changing to most-changing. Copy dependency manifests (package.json, requirements.txt) first, install dependencies, then copy application code.

errors.name: Docker Daemon Not Running
errors.message: Cannot connect to the Docker daemon at unix:///var/run/docker.sock. Is the docker daemon running?
errors.cause: The Docker daemon process is not started, or the current user does not have permission to access the socket.
errors.fix: Start Docker: `sudo systemctl start docker`. Add user to docker group: `sudo usermod -aG docker $USER`. On Desktop, open the Docker application.

errors.name: Docker Daemon Out of Memory
errors.message: Error response from daemon: cannot allocate memory
errors.cause: The Docker host does not have enough free memory to start the container.
errors.fix: Stop unused containers: `docker stop $(docker ps -q)`. Increase host RAM. Set container memory limits to avoid any single container consuming all available memory.

errors.name: Container Name Already in Use
errors.message: Error response from daemon: Conflict. The container name "/myapp" is already in use
errors.cause: A stopped container with the same name still exists.
errors.fix: Remove the existing container: `docker rm myapp`. Or use `--rm` flag so the container auto-removes on exit. Or use `docker rename` to keep the old one.

errors.name: Container Health Check Failing
errors.message: container is unhealthy
errors.cause: The HEALTHCHECK instruction in the Dockerfile is failing. The application may not be ready or crashed silently.
errors.fix: Inspect health logs: `docker inspect --format='{{json .State.Health}}' <container>`. Adjust `--health-start-period` to give the app more startup time. Debug the healthcheck command manually.

errors.name: Compose File Version Unsupported
errors.message: Version in "./docker-compose.yml" is unsupported
errors.cause: The Compose file specifies a version that the installed Docker Compose version does not support.
errors.fix: Update Docker Compose to the latest version. Or lower the `version:` field in docker-compose.yml. Compose v2 (bundled with Docker) uses the Compose Specification and ignores the version field.

errors.name: Compose Service Failed to Start
errors.message: service <name> failed to build or start
errors.cause: One of the services in a docker-compose.yml failed during build or startup.
errors.fix: Run `docker compose logs <service>` to isolate the failing service. Start services individually with `docker compose up <service>` to narrow down the issue.

errors.name: Service Started Before Dependency Ready
errors.message: Connection refused / service not available on startup
errors.cause: `depends_on` only waits for the container to start, not for the service inside to be ready. A database container starting does not mean Postgres is accepting connections yet.
errors.fix: Add a `healthcheck` to the dependency service and use `depends_on: condition: service_healthy` in Compose v3.9+. Or add a startup retry loop in the dependent application.

errors.name: Docker Hub Rate Limit
errors.message: toomanyrequests: Too Many Requests. Please see https://docs.docker.com/docker-hub/download-rate-limit/
errors.cause: Anonymous Docker Hub pulls are rate-limited to 100 pulls per 6 hours per IP. CI/CD environments sharing an IP can hit this quickly.
errors.fix: Login to Docker Hub in CI: `docker login -u $DOCKER_USER -p $DOCKER_TOKEN`. Use a Docker Hub Pro account. Mirror images to a private registry. Use `--cache-from` to reduce pull frequency.

errors.name: TLS Certificate Error
errors.message: x509: certificate signed by unknown authority
errors.cause: Attempting to connect to a private registry using a self-signed certificate that Docker does not trust.
errors.fix: Add the CA cert to `/etc/docker/certs.d/<registry>/ca.crt`. Or add the registry to `insecure-registries` in `/etc/docker/daemon.json` (not recommended for production).

errors.name: Exec Format Error
errors.message: exec format error
errors.cause: The binary in the container was compiled for a different architecture than the host. Common when building on Apple Silicon (arm64) and deploying to x86_64 servers.
errors.fix: Build for the target platform: `docker buildx build --platform linux/amd64 -t myimage .`. Use multi-stage builds to compile inside the container rather than copying host binaries.

errors.name: Operation Not Permitted (Capabilities)
errors.message: Operation not permitted / EPERM
errors.cause: The container is attempting a privileged operation (e.g. binding port < 1024, modifying kernel parameters) that requires Linux capabilities not granted by default.
errors.fix: Grant specific capabilities with `--cap-add NET_BIND_SERVICE` rather than running `--privileged`. For sysctl changes use `--sysctl net.ipv4.ip_forward=1`.

errors.name: Syscall Blocked by Seccomp
errors.message: Operation not permitted (seccomp profile blocked syscall)
errors.cause: Docker's default seccomp profile blocks certain syscalls. Some applications (perf, certain JVM operations, ptrace) require syscalls not in the default allowlist.
errors.fix: Run with a custom seccomp profile: `--security-opt seccomp=custom.json`. As a last resort: `--security-opt seccomp=unconfined` (not recommended for production).

errors.name: cgroup Resource Limit Exceeded
errors.message: container killed due to resource limit
errors.cause: Container exceeded CPU, memory, or PID limits set by cgroup constraints (via Docker `--memory`, `--cpus`, `--pids-limit` flags or Kubernetes resource limits).
errors.fix: Increase limits: `--memory=2g --cpus=1`. Profile the application for resource leaks. Use `docker stats <container>` to monitor live resource usage.

errors.name: COPY / ADD Source Not Found
errors.message: COPY failed: file not found in build context or excluded by .dockerignore
errors.cause: The file specified in a COPY instruction does not exist in the build context, or it was excluded by .dockerignore.
errors.fix: Check the file path relative to the build context directory. Review .dockerignore for accidental exclusions. Verify the file exists locally before building.

errors.name: Image Layer Too Large
errors.message: Image size is unexpectedly large
errors.cause: Files added in one RUN layer are deleted in a subsequent layer, but the data still exists in the earlier layer (layers are additive).
errors.fix: Combine RUN commands with `&&` and clean up in the same layer: `RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y curl && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*`. Use multi-stage builds.

errors.name: Log Driver Configuration Error
errors.message: Error starting logging driver
errors.cause: The configured log driver (fluentd, awslogs, gelf) is unreachable or misconfigured.
errors.fix: Verify the log driver endpoint is reachable from the Docker host. Check log driver options in daemon.json or container `--log-driver` and `--log-opt` flags.

errors.name: OverlayFS Error
errors.message: Error response from daemon: driver "overlay2" failed to create layer
errors.cause: OverlayFS storage driver error, often caused by filesystem incompatibility (e.g. XFS without d_type support, ZFS, NFS).
errors.fix: Check filesystem type: `df -T /var/lib/docker`. Ensure XFS is formatted with `ftype=1`. Consider switching to a supported storage driver for your filesystem.

errors.name: Swarm Not Initialized
errors.message: Error response from daemon: This node is not a swarm manager
errors.cause: Attempting to run Swarm commands on a node that has not been initialized as a Swarm manager.
errors.fix: Initialize Swarm: `docker swarm init`. For worker nodes, use the join token from `docker swarm join-token worker`.

errors.name: Network Subnet Conflict
errors.message: Error response from daemon: Pool overlaps with other one on this address space
errors.cause: The subnet Docker is trying to allocate for a new network overlaps with an existing network or host route.
errors.fix: Specify a custom subnet: `docker network create --subnet 172.28.0.0/16 mynet`. Or prune unused networks: `docker network prune`.

errors.name: Health Check Timeout
errors.message: health_status: unhealthy (timeout)
errors.cause: The healthcheck command took longer than the configured `--health-timeout` value (default 30s).
errors.fix: Increase timeout: `HEALTHCHECK --interval=30s --timeout=60s CMD ...`. Optimize the health check command. Ensure the application is not under excessive load.

errors.name: Required Environment Variable Missing
errors.message: Application crash on startup: environment variable <VAR> not set
errors.cause: The application requires an environment variable that was not passed to the container.
errors.fix: Pass env vars with `-e VAR=value` or `--env-file .env`. In Compose, use the `environment:` or `env_file:` keys. Validate required vars in entrypoint scripts with `set -u`.

errors.name: File Permission Denied Inside Container
errors.message: Permission denied writing to /app or /data
errors.cause: The process inside the container is running as a non-root user without write permission to the target directory.
errors.fix: Add `RUN chown -R appuser:appuser /app` before switching to the non-root user in your Dockerfile. Or set directory permissions with `RUN chmod 755 /data`.

errors.name: Zombie Processes (PID 1 Problem)
errors.message: Process accumulation / container not responding to SIGTERM
errors.cause: When a shell script is PID 1 in a container, it does not reap child processes or forward signals correctly, causing zombie accumulation and unclean shutdowns.
errors.fix: Use `exec` in entrypoint scripts: `exec node server.js`. Or use a lightweight init system: `docker run --init`. Or use `tini` as PID 1 in the Dockerfile.

errors.name: Open File Limit Exceeded (ulimit)
errors.message: Too many open files
errors.cause: The process inside the container hit the open file descriptor limit. Common with high-traffic servers, databases, or applications that open many connections.
errors.fix: Increase limits: `docker run --ulimit nofile=65535:65535`. Set system-wide defaults in `/etc/docker/daemon.json` under `"default-ulimits"`. Check current usage: `ls /proc/<pid>/fd | wc -l`.
